edge_hypothesis: CCI (Commodity Channel Index) has NEVER been tested in any strategy across 706+ outcomes. CCI oscillates around 0 and crosses +/-100 frequently — making it a CONTINUOUS ZONE-BASED signal. This directly addresses the trade-count problem that killed Donchian channel strategies (0 trades because level-touch signals are too rare). The thesis: CCI beyond +/-100 indicates EXHAUSTION — price has moved too far from its statistical mean. In RANGING markets (CHOP > 50), these exhaustion readings mean-revert because no trend is present to sustain the extreme move. The CHOP gate is critical: without it, CCI < -100 during a downtrend is just a momentum confirmation (NOT exhaustion). With CHOP > 50, CCI < -100 means price has spiked below the range floor and will likely revert to the range midpoint. This is the same mean-reversion thesis that produced 19/24 unique ACCEPTs in ranging regimes, but using a COMPLETELY UNTESTED indicator family. THREE OUTCOMES: (1) PF > 1.2 with 50+ trades: CCI is a viable exhaustion signal — iterate with tighter/wider bands and different CHOP thresholds. (2) PF < 1.0 with 50+ trades: CCI exhaustion doesn't predict direction in ranging markets — the indicator measures something other than reversion potential. (3) Trade count < 20: CHOP > 50 is too permissive or too restrictive — calibrate threshold. INVALIDATION: If PF < 0.8 across all variants, CCI exhaustion is NOT a tradeable signal in crypto ranges.

variants.name: cci_chop_fade_1h_8to1
variants.description: CCI exhaustion fade with CHOP ranging gate on ETH/1h. Entry when CCI crosses beyond +/-100 in a confirmed ranging market (CHOP > 50). 8:1 R:R matching proven ACCEPT profile. First-ever CCI strategy test. Expected: 40-120 trades (CCI crosses +/-100 frequently on 1h). CCI is a continuous oscillator — when it's below -100, it stays there for multiple bars, giving multiple entry opportunities. This is fundamentally different from Donchian touches which are single-bar events.
filters: CCI exhaustion: CCI_20_0.015 beyond +/-100 = exhaustion signal
CHOP ranging gate: CHOP_14_1_100 > 50 = confirmed range-bound market
Mean-reversion in ranges: fade CCI extremes during choppy markets
First-ever CCI test in the system
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: CCI_20_0.015 beyond +/-100 = exhaustion entry. First-ever test of CCI in the system.
CHOP_14_1_100 > 50 = ranging market gate. Ensures mean-reversion context.

variants.name: cci_chop_fade_4h_8to1
variants.description: CCI exhaustion fade on ETH/4h. Same thesis as 1h variant but on higher timeframe. 4h CCI extremes represent larger price dislocations — each exhaustion event is more significant. Expected: 15-50 trades (4h reduces frequency). If 4h PF > 1h PF, it confirms that larger-timeframe exhaustion readings produce stronger mean-reversion. If trade count < 15, the 4h signal is too infrequent for 8:1 R:R — need to widen CCI threshold to +/-80.
filters: 4h timeframe: larger exhaustion events, higher signal quality
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: CCI_20_0.015 beyond +/-100 on 4h — larger dislocation per signal.

variants.name: cci_chop_fade_1h_relaxed_6to1
variants.description: CCI exhaustion with RELAXED threshold (CCI +/-80 instead of +/-100) and 6:1 R:R. The diagnostic variant: if CCI > 100 produces too few trades, CCI > 80 casts a wider net while still indicating above-normal extension. 6:1 R:R (1.5x / 9.0x) increases win rate requirement to 14.3% but captures more moderate mean-reversion moves that don't travel the full 12x ATR. Expected: 80-200 trades. If PF > 1.0 at CCI 80 but PF < 1.0 at CCI 100: the sweet spot is between 80-100. If PF < 1.0 at both: CCI exhaustion is not a viable signal.
filters: CCI relaxed threshold: CCI_20_0.015 beyond +/-80 (vs +/-100 in base variant)
Diagnostic: does relaxing CCI threshold improve trade count without killing PF?
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 6:1. Needs 14.3% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: CCI_20_0.015 beyond +/-80 — relaxed threshold for more signals.
